label: Boss entry and formation grammar conformance
intent: Measure whether boss entry, escort composition, formation settling, regular-stage alien entry variation, and challenge-stage set pieces are evolving as recognizable Galaga-like choreography rather than generic repeated waves.

bestCase: stage-specific reference contact sheets with boss and escort entry labels
stage-specific reference contact sheets for alien entry formations, entry side, rack targets, and stage-to-stage novelty
frame-level path traces for boss, escort, and challenge-stage formations
challenge-stage reference labels for new alien introduction, trajectory family, bonus opportunity, and result feedback
rack slot coordinates at formation settle
expert-route score/reward outcomes for boss opportunity windows

metrics.label: Boss entry timing
metrics.success: Regular-stage boss appearances land in stage-band timing windows and vary enough over time to feel authored.

metrics.label: Boss escort composition
metrics.success: Mid/late windows include visible boss and escort relationships rather than isolated boss dives only.

metrics.label: Formation settle evidence
metrics.success: Evidence captures formation-active counts and, in the next scorer version, target rack slot geometry by stage.

metrics.label: Challenge-stage pattern identity
metrics.success: Challenge stages show wave start, path, hit, result, boss/family variety, and bonus-learning opportunities.

metrics.label: Stage-to-stage formation variation
metrics.success: Nearby regular stages maintain signature distance above the repetition-risk threshold and show visibly different alien entry formations, rack timing, and path methods.

metrics.label: Path shape and set-formation precision
metrics.success: Runtime boss/escort/challenge path traces and formation slot positions are available first, then promoted to direct reference comparison against Galaga path families.

longCycleTrack.label: Alien entry and challenge-stage novelty
longCycleTrack.problem: Aurora can satisfy broad boss/formation checks while still feeling non-conformant because regular-stage entries repeat too much, challenging-stage trajectory families are too sparse, and new alien introduction is not yet measured as a learnable bonus-stage event.
longCycleTrack.strategy: Run measured extraction first: broaden early/mid/late regular entry windows and several challenge-stage windows, label alien family, entry side, rack target, trajectory family, novelty role, and bonus opportunity, then tune stage scripts against the weakest measured gap.
longCycleTrack.successMeasure: Before a major release gate, dedicated alien-entry/challenge-variation scoring should exceed 7.5/10, regular-stage minimum signature distance should exceed 0.22, at least four challenge-stage windows should be scored, and path precision should be grounded by Galaga-family reference contact sheets rather than heuristic runtime families only.

releaseUse: This family should appear as a key Level Arc / Formation Grammar gate before any major Aurora release that claims a meaningful Galaga conformance lift.
